perturbation.original_text: Melamine Chemicals, Inc. ("MCI") and Borden Chemical, Inc. (the "Company") are prepared to engage in discussions with respect to a possible negotiated business combination involving the Company and MCI (the "Transaction"), and during the course of such discussions MCI may disclose and make available to the Company certain information concerning MCI's business, prospects, financial condition, operations, technology, assets and liabilities.
perturbation.changed_text: Melamine Chemicals, Inc. ("MCI") and Borden Chemical, Inc. (the "Company") are prepared to engage in discussions with respect to a possible negotiated business combination involving the Company and MCI (the "Negotiation"), and during the course of such discussions MCI may disclose and make available to the Company certain information concerning MCI's business, prospects, financial condition, operations, technology, assets and liabilities.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Transaction' is being replaced by the term 'Negotiation'. This introduces a contradiction, because 'Transaction' is specifically defined for the whole agreement. Therefore, every time it appears somewhere else it will be referring to 'Negotiation' creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All such information furnished to the Company or its Representatives (as defined below) by or on behalf of MCI (irrespective of the form of communication and whether such information is furnished prior to, on or after the date hereof), and all analyses, compilations, data, studies, notes, interpretations, memoranda or other documents prepared by the Company or its Representatives containing or based in whole or in part on any such furnished information are collectively referred to herein as the "Confidential Information." As a condition to being furnished the Confidential Information, the Company agrees as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the term 'Transaction' with 'Negotiation'. The explanation states that 'Transaction' is specifically defined for the whole agreement. The contradicted text uses 'Transaction' and, because of the changed text, will now be implicitly referencing 'Negotiation' instead. This creates a direct conflict because the agreement now refers to 'Negotiation' instead of the defined 'Transaction' where 'Confidential Information' is related to, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. (a) the Company shall (i) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no other competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, officers, employees and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers and attorneys) acting on <PAGE> its behalf (such directors, officers, employees and representatives being referred to hereinafter collectively as its "Representatives") who in each case, in the Company's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction; provided, however, that prospective financing sources shall not be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed in accordance with this paragraph 1 unless specifically requested by the Company and consented to by MCI in writing;
perturbation.changed_text: NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. (a) the Company shall (i) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Negotiation and for no other competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, officers, employees and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers and attorneys) acting on <PAGE> its behalf (such directors, officers, employees and representatives being referred to hereinafter collectively as its "Representatives") who in each case, in the Company's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Solicitation; provided, however, that prospective financing sources shall not be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed in accordance with this paragraph 1 unless specifically requested by the Company and consented to by MCI in writing;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Transaction' is being replaced by the term 'Negotiation' and 'Solicitation'. This introduces a contradiction, because 'Transaction' is specifically defined for the whole agreement. Therefore, every time it appears somewhere else it will be referring to 'Negotiation' creating an in-text contradiction. Also, it is referring to 'Solicitation' which is no where to be found in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. (a) the Company shall (i) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Negotiation and for no other competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, officers, employees and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers and attorneys) acting on <PAGE> its behalf (such directors, officers, employees and representatives being referred to hereinafter collectively as its "Representatives") who in each case, in the Company's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Solicitation; provided, however, that prospective financing sources shall not be considered "Representatives" to whom Confidential Information may be disclosed in accordance with this paragraph 1 unless specifically requested by the Company and consented to by MCI in writing;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Transaction' with 'Negotiation' and introduces 'Solicitation,' while the explanation indicates 'Transaction' has a specific, defined meaning within the agreement. This creates a strong contradiction because subsequent uses of 'Transaction' in other sections would now implicitly refer to 'Negotiation,' altering the intended meaning and obligations. The introduction of 'Solicitation,' a term not previously defined, further exacerbates the contradiction as its meaning is unclear, causing confusion and potential unenforceability. The contradicted text highlights the direct impact of this misalignment, creating a clear conflict and inconsistency within the contract, fitting the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: NON-DISCLOSURE OF NEGOTIATIONS OR AGREEMENTS. Except as required by law, neither the Company nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements concerning a possible Transaction, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between the Company and MCI with respect thereto, or that the Company has received from MCI Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other, which consent will not be withheld unreasonably.
perturbation.changed_text: NON-DISCLOSURE OF NEGOTIATIONS OR AGREEMENTS. Except as required by law, neither the Company nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements concerning a possible Contract, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between the Company and MCI with respect thereto, or that the Company has received from MCI Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other, which consent will not be withheld unreasonably.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Transaction' with 'Contract' creates ambiguity. 'Transaction' is defined, but 'Contract' isn't in this section, leading to uncertainty about whether they refer to the same thing. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the contract refers to Transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE OF NEGOTIATIONS OR AGREEMENTS. Except as required by law, neither the Company nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements concerning a possible Contract, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between the Company and MCI with respect thereto, or that the Company has received from MCI Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other, which consent will not be withheld unreasonably.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Transaction,' which is assumed to be a defined term within the contract. The changed text replaces 'Transaction' with 'Contract,' which is not explicitly defined in the provided section. The contradiction arises because the 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Contract'. Since Transaction and Contract may not be the same thing, and 'Transaction' is the term defined and used in the original agreement, changing it to 'Contract' introduces ambiguity and contradicts the document's consistent use of 'Transaction' to refer to a specific action or agreement. 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' applies here because using the terms interchangeably creates conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, the Company and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the confidentiality and other obligations hereunder, until the expiration of three years from the date of this Agreement, unless a lesser duration is specified herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Private Information, the Company and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the confidentiality and other obligations hereunder, until the expiration of three years from the date of this Agreement, unless a lesser duration is specified herein.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Private Information'. This introduces a significant contradiction, as 'Confidential Information' is specifically defined in the document. By using 'Private Information' instead, it's now unclear what specific information the Company is obligated to keep confidential, therefore there will be in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Private Information, the Company and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the confidentiality and other obligations hereunder, until the expiration of three years from the date of this Agreement, unless a lesser duration is specified herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' which, according to the explanation, is specifically defined within the contract. The changed text replaces this with 'Private Information'. The contradicted text also uses the term 'Private Information'. Since 'Private Information' is not a defined term, and 'Confidential Information' is, this creates ambiguity about the scope of the confidentiality obligations, causing an in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'
